mnemonics.title: Gradient of a scalar field
mnemonics.content: A vector field that points in the direction of the greatest rate of change of the scalar field.

mnemonics.title: Divergence of a vector field
mnemonics.content: A scalar field that represents the rate at which the vector field is flowing away from a point.

mnemonics.title: Curl of a vector field
mnemonics.content: A vector field that represents the rotation of the vector field around a point.

mnemonics.title: Laplacian of a scalar field
mnemonics.content: A scalar field that represents the rate at which the scalar field is changing around a point.

acronyms.expansion: Size, Color, Alignment, Length, Angle, Rotation
acronyms.usage: To remember the properties of a scalar field.

acronyms.expansion: Velocity, Electric field, Curl, Orientation, Rotation
acronyms.usage: To remember the properties of a vector field.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a hill with a ball rolling down it. The ball will roll in the direction of the greatest rate of change of the height of the hill.
Imagine a fluid flowing out of a point. The divergence of the vector field represents the rate at which the fluid is flowing away from the point.
Imagine a rotating wheel. The curl of the vector field represents the rotation of the wheel around a point.
Imagine a heat map. The Laplacian of the scalar field represents the rate at which the heat is changing around a point.

analogies.analogy: The gradient of a scalar field is like a compass that points in the direction of the greatest rate of change of the scalar field.
The divergence of a vector field is like a faucet that controls the flow of water out of a point.
The curl of a vector field is like a drill that rotates around a point.
The Laplacian of a scalar field is like a thermometer that measures the rate at which the temperature is changing around a point.
